figures.caption: Table 1: Model 1: Yt+1 = sin(Yt) + et+1 with normal innovations.
Table 2: Model 1: Yt+1 = sin(Yt) + et+1 with Laplace innovations.
Table 3: Model 2: Yt+1 = 0.8 log(3Y 2 t + 1) + et+1 with normal innovations.
Table 4: Model 2: Yt+1 = 0.8 log(3Y 2 t + 1) + et+1 with Laplace innovations.
Figure 1: The conditional coverage rate and interval length for different 95%-PIs with simulated data from Model 1 with normal error.
Figure 2: The conditional coverage rate and interval length for different 95%-PIs with simulated data from Model 2 with normal error.
Table 5: The out-of-sample prediction performance of different PIs on log-returns of S&P500 price index with 100 or 250 rolling window size.
Figure 3: The histogram of length for rolling 90%- and 95%-PIs with log-returns of S&P500 price index when w = 250.
Figure 4: The conditional coverage rate and interval length for different 95%-PIs with simulated data from Model 1 with laplace error.
Figure 5: The conditional coverage rate and interval length for different 95%-PIs with simulated data from Model 2 with laplace error.
